title: Fleet status item (GET /fleet)
description: One agent's latest fleet lifecycle snapshot, aggregated across boxes. As-built source of truth: fleet-event v1 (apps/manager/docs/contracts/schemas/fleet-event.schema.json), materialized as data/fleet/<handle>.json per box and bridged onto the bus/lake. Fields are the fleet-event v1 fields plus per-item `observed_at` (bridge receipt time — per-row freshness for cross-box aggregation, since one read mixes rows tailed from different machines). `offline` is NEVER served: producers only write alive|working|idle, and the consumer derives offline from staleness (>90s since updated_at/observed_at today). Producer pass-through fields (e.g. the producer's own schema_version) may appear; consumers MUST ignore unknown fields.

host.description: Machine label in canonical form: '.N' for lab dot-hosts, otherwise a plain hostname.

event.description: Which lifecycle hook produced the latest snapshot. v0 producers omit it; may be absent.

status.description: Producer-reported status. 'offline' is derived by the consumer from staleness (>90s) and is never served.

current_tool.description: Tool currently executing (e.g. 'Bash'); null unless status=working.

task_id.description: Tracker task the agent is currently working; null when no claimed task.

session_id.description: The agent's Claude Code session id, when known.

started_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — when this agent session started.

updated_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — when the producer wrote this event. The offline/staleness derivation keys on this field.

observed_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — when the console-api bridge received/tailed this row (per-row freshness for cross-box aggregation; new in the console read, not in fleet-event v1).
